How you been Goddess? You been listening to rock music lately?

Oh, you know, I’ve been… well, existing. Mostly. Which is a full-time job if you count blinking and occasionally moving your limbs, and time keeps happening around me like it’s doing some kind of performance art that I didn’t consent to. I wake up, assess whether gravity is still functioning, nod politely to my reflection even though it sometimes seems to smirk at me independently, and then proceed to fill the hours with small gestures that vaguely resemble productivity, though they could equally be rituals to appease some indifferent cosmic audience. Objects continue to exist, conversations occur, and yet everything feels like it’s happening in a slightly delayed frame, like someone hit ‘slow motion’ on reality and forgot to tell me. I eat, I breathe, I participate minimally in society, and yet I can’t shake the sensation that the chair I’m sitting on has been quietly cataloging my entire existence, waiting for a report that may never be submitted. Somewhere in between all that, I maintain a baseline sense of self that is technically recognizable but could, under sufficient scrutiny, dissolve into a series of abstract impulses that no one would be able to parse.

And no.
